# HYBE Executives Land 30th in Billboard's 2026 Power 100

Multi-home, multi-genre strategy and global influence recognized in Leaderboard category

By OSEN

Published 2026.01.29. 09:39

![](https://www.chosun.com/resizer/v2/GQ4TMZBZHEZDCODFMI4GCOBZGY.jpg?auth=2a1f344a1814646ffd73e5b71ce8b6d8d1ad37899fc4c5d37d8669250a6869a2&width=530&height=691&smart=true)

[OSEN=Choi Lee-jung Reporter] HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk, HYBE CEO Lee Jae-sang, and HYBE America Chairman and CEO Isaac Lee were all named to ‘Billboard’s 2026 Power 100.’

The U.S. music media outlet Billboard announced the list of the world’s most influential figures in the music industry, ‘Billboard’s 2026 Power 100,’ on its official website on the 28th (local time). This year’s list featured Chairman Bang, CEO Lee, and Chairman and CEO Isaac Lee in the ‘Leaderboard’ category, which recognizes 40 leaders who have significantly impacted the global music industry, ranking them jointly at 30th place. This reaffirms HYBE’s strong presence in the global entertainment market.

Billboard described HYBE, stating, “Through its ‘multi-home, multi-genre’ strategy, it has established overseas bases and utilized K-pop-style artist and fandom development methods in the global market.” Chairman Bang and the other two HYBE executives were also ranked 5th in the ‘Multisector’ category, which comprehensively evaluates influence across various aspects of music production and consumption, including management and performances.

Chairman Bang has been selected for the list six times, including this year, following his appearances in 2020, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025. Billboard highlighted, “HYBE does not merely export artists produced in Korea to overseas markets, including the U.S.,” and praised Chairman Bang’s K-pop methodology for establishing itself as a localized success model in major global markets.

Notably, Billboard mentioned that KATSEYE, a global girl group applying Chairman Bang’s K-pop methodology, “was nominated for ‘Best New Artist’ at the Grammys through a collaboration with Geffen Records.” KATSEYE also received a simultaneous nomination in the ‘Best Pop Duo/Group Performance’ category, solidifying its position as a global leading group.

CEO Lee Jae-sang and Chairman and CEO Isaac Lee, who were named to the list for the first time this year, also drew attention. This is interpreted as recognition of their achievements in securing future growth engines for HYBE and advancing its global business.

CEO Lee has led the ‘HYBE 2.0’ strategy, driving preemptive innovation in the industry by integrating content and technology, and has provided fans with new immersive experiences. He also accelerated the ‘multi-home, multi-genre’ strategy by establishing HYBE China and HYBE India last year. Thanks to these achievements, he was selected as one of the most influential entertainment executives worldwide by Billboard’s 2025 Power Players and Variety, a U.S. entertainment magazine.

Billboard noted, “HYBE established regional bases in China and India, the two most populous countries in the world,” and highlighted the achievements of HYBE Music Group artists, including SEVENTEEN’s regular 5th album ‘HAPPY BURSTDAY’ topping the Billboard Top Album Sales chart last year, and Tomorrow X Together and ENHYPEN ranking 3rd on the Billboard 200.

Chairman and CEO Isaac Lee has contributed to expanding HYBE’s musical territory by leading its global strategy in the North American and Latin markets. Billboard highlighted his achievement, stating, “In Latin America, he created the boy band Santos Bravos through a reality series.” Santos Bravos, which debuted in October last year following the reality series of the same name, is evaluated as a successful fusion of the K-pop system and Latin pop. Chairman and CEO Isaac Lee was also selected for Billboard’s Latin Power Players 2025 last year.

Meanwhile, Billboard’s Power 100 is an annual list published by Billboard recognizing influential figures in the global music industry, selecting leaders in categories such as labels, publishing, and multisector. This year’s list also included Lucian Grainge, CEO of UMG (Universal Music Group), Rob Stringer, Chairman of Sony Music Group, and Michael Rapino, Chairman of Live Nation.

# As K-pop superstars BTS return, HYBE CEO to test ‘scarcity’ model for fandom business

HYBE, the South Korean entertainment giant behind BTS – arguably the world’s biggest K-pop act – is planning to spend 2026 developing “a new experience model based on scarcity,” according to company CEO Jason Jaesang Lee.

“Scarcity is an important element that enhances the added value of the fan experience,” Lee said in a New Year address to HYBE staff, as reported in media sources in South Korea.

“To innovate future fandom businesses, we will design and test an integrated online and offline experience model based on scarcity.” [...] “If 2025 was a year of investment to lay the groundwork for ‘HYBE 2.0,’ then 2026 will mark the beginning of visible results. This year, we will prove to the market that our bold investments were the right direction,” Lee said in his New Year’s address.

Yet for fans, the largest change at HYBE this year will no doubt be the return of BTS, a musical phenomenon so strongly linked to HYBE’s fortunes that the company’s stock plummeted when BTS announced their hiatus back in 2022. The stock then rallied in the summer of 2025 when BTS members appeared together at HYBE HQ with a “we are back” banner in the background.

Yet, as Lee’s message indicates, the HYBE of today is moving beyond being “the label behind BTS,” and even moving beyond K-pop itself.

## Music Business Worldwide

# As K-pop superstars BTS return, HYBE CEO to test ‘scarcity’ model for fandom business

![]()

HYBE, the South Korean entertainment giant behind BTS – arguably the world’s biggest K-pop act – is planning to spend 2026 developing “a new experience model based on scarcity,” according to company CEO Jason Jaesang Lee.

“Scarcity is an important element that enhances the added value of the fan experience,” Lee said in a New Year address to HYBE staff, as [reported in media sources](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/life-general/2026010210453259652) in South Korea.

“To innovate future fandom businesses, we will design and test an integrated online and offline experience model based on scarcity.”

Lee seemingly offered few other details on what the “scarcity” model might look like. Fewer concerts and appearances? VIP-only access to events? Limited access to new releases? But one thing is certain: Increased scarcity drives up prices, and that means potential new revenue growth opportunities.

Whatever HYBE’s plans may be, Lee’s scarcity philosophy is about to be tested with the return of BTS, the global superstar K-pop boy group that has been quite scarce for the past four years as its members completed mandatory military training.

HYBE on Tuesday (January 13) officially [unveiled tour dates](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/bts-world-tour-the-biggest-global-k-pop-tour-of-all-time-to-span-79-shows-across-34-regions/) for BTS’s first world tour in four years, set to begin on April 9 with a three-night engagement at Goyang Stadium near Seoul. The tour will span five continents with **79 shows** in **34 markets**.

That follows last week’s [announcement](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/bts-set-march-album-release-first-world-tour-in-four-years/) that a new BTS album will land on March 20.

In what might be a taste of one element of HYBE’s ‘scarcity’ model, members of BTS’ fan club, ARMY, will have access to a ticket presale for 36 of the shows starting on January 22. Two more Los Angeles shows will be available to ARMY members on January 23. The tour’s general onsale will begin January 24 at LiveNation.com.

With BTS out of the limelight for four years, HYBE has become [less reliant](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/bts-once-hybes-top-earner-accounted-for-below-20-of-the-k-pop-giants-revenues-in-2024-down-from-95-at-their-peak/) on the superstar group for its revenue, and while the BTS boys were busy with their military training, HYBE was busy with major expansions [in the US](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-launches-us-based-services-arm-with-a-global-focus-and-its-working-with-independent-artists-and-labels/), [Latin America](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/via-the-medellin-music-lab-hybe-latin-america-is-betting-on-colombia-as-a-hotspot-for-global-music-industry-talent/), and [Africa](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-to-build-a-global-platform-for-african-talent-via-new-partnership-with-tyla-managers-brandon-hixon-and-colin-gayle/).

That has included major investments into the acquisition of local music labels and the establishment of new global musical acts like [**KATSEYE**](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/katseye-named-tiktoks-global-artist-of-the-year-for-2025/) (formed in partnership with **[Universal Music Group](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/companies/universal-music-group/ "Companies > Universal Music Group [4,611 articles]")**‘s **Geffen Records**). In 2026, CEO Lee told HYBE staff, the company aims to turn those investments into profit.

![](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/files/2026/01/Screenshot-2026-01-14-at-11.58.01-80x82.jpg)

“To innovate future fandom businesses, we will design and test an integrated online and offline experience model based on scarcity.”

Jason Jaesang Lee, HYBE

It has also meant the development of **Weverse**, HYBE’s platform for superfans that has been [attracting](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/ariana-grande-officially-joins-hybes-weverse-superfan-app1/) Western [artists](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/megan-thee-stallion-joins-weverse-as-hybe-revamps-superfan-platform-to-include-advanced-translation-features-for-artist-posts/) with the promise of a stronger connection with fans and new monetization opportunities.

2026 will be the year HYBE needs to show those investments were worthwhile, Lee told HYBE staff in his address.

“We need to prove the justification for investment in new businesses and artist IP where preemptive investment has been made,” he said, as [quoted by](https://biz.chosun.com/en/en-industry/2026/01/02/4BQSIDXVKZHUXKULDCGH7IZJMM/) *Chosun Daily*.

“We have confirmed meaningful traffic expansion so far, and now we must transition to a phase of valid revenue generation to be confident in sustainable business viability,” Lee said, adding that “when investment shows results, new investment gains legitimacy and a virtuous cycle of growth is established.”

Saying that securing profitability is HYBE’s top priority for 2026, Lee laid out five tasks he says HYBE has to accomplish this year, [per](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/life-general/2026010210453259652) *Asia Business Daily*:

Lee said this marks the “realization” of the plans laid out under the HYBE 2.0 strategy that he [announced](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-the-home-of-bts-restructures-global-business-as-part-of-new-strategy-called-hybe-2-0/) shortly after being [appointed CEO](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-ceo-jiwon-park-resigns-chief-strategy-officer-jason-jaesang-lee-named-as-new-chief-exec-at-k-pop-giant12/) in 2024. The mid- and long-term strategy involves shifting the company from its initial “label-solution-platform” structure to a “music-platform-tech” structure.

So far, the “music” part of the new structure has seen, among other things, the reorganization of HYBE’s labels in South Korea and Japan under the new **HYBE Music Group APAC**; the “platform” part has seen the continued expansion of features on the Weverse platform; and the “tech” part has HYBE exploring and investing in new [AI-driven technologies](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybes-ai-voice-cloning-company-supertone-unveils-text-to-speech-tool-that-can-create-a-custom-voice-in-just-10-seconds/).

“If 2025 was a year of investment to lay the groundwork for ‘HYBE 2.0,’ then 2026 will mark the beginning of visible results. This year, we will prove to the market that our bold investments were the right direction,” Lee said in his New Year’s address.

Yet for fans, the largest change at HYBE this year will no doubt be the return of BTS, a musical phenomenon so strongly linked to HYBE’s fortunes that the company’s stock [plummeted](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-share-price-plummets-as-bts-announce-break-but-could-bands-hiatus-actually-be-good-news-for-the-korean-company/) when BTS announced their hiatus back in 2022. The stock then [rallied](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-stock-rallies-to-three-year-high-as-bts-members-complete-military-service/) in the summer of 2025 when BTS members appeared together at HYBE HQ with a “we are back” banner in the background.

Yet, as Lee’s message indicates, the HYBE of today is moving beyond being “the label behind BTS,” and even moving beyond K-pop itself.

“HYBE’s growth philosophy is clear: to create top-tier IP, lead the fandom business, and expand those results into new markets and genres,” Lee said.

In the meantime, the music industry will be watching closely to see just how HYBE’s “scarcity” strategy pans out.Music Business Worldwide

# Hybe leaders named to Billboard’s 2026 Power 100 list

Published : Jan. 29, 2026 - 16:16:24
Updated : Jan. 30, 2026 - 18:13:34

Link copied!

![Lee Jung-joo](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/svc/user/707a4c0d-5737-44db-a5ed-b059869a5240.jpg)

Covering K-pop issues,  
trends

![From left: chairman Bang Si-hyuk, CEO Lee Jae-sang and Hybe America chairman and CEO Isaac Lee (Hybe)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/news/cms/2026/01/30/news-p.v1.20260129.f5dcf41d7ae1482293d8d359026b1c81_P1.png)

Hybe’s top executives — chairman Bang Si-hyuk, CEO Lee Jae-sang and Hybe America chairman and CEO Isaac Lee — have been named among the most influential figures in the global music industry, according to Billboard’s 2026 Power 100 list released Wednesday.

Billboard annually selects key leaders across the global music ecosystem for its Power 100 list, recognizing those driving innovation across sectors such as labels, publishing, live performance and multi-sector leadership. The three leaders of Hybe were jointly listed at No. 30 on this year’s “Leaderboard” — a category spotlighting 40 global executives who are reshaping the music business.

Billboard praised Hybe’s “multihome, multigenre” strategy, highlighting how the company has successfully established international agencies while applying its K-pop-style artist and fandom development model to the global music market. It spotlighted Katesye — Hybe’s girl group co-developed with Geffen Records — as a prime example of that approach, noting the group’s Grammy nomination for Best New Artist.

Bang’s presence on the list marks his sixth appearance overall, having previously been selected in 2020 and consecutively from 2022 to 2025.

CEO Lee Jae-sang and Hybe America head Isaac Lee marked their first appearance on the Power 100 this year, recognized for leading Hybe’s global expansion as well as identifying future growth engines.

Lee Jae-sang, who spearheaded Hybe’s “Hybe 2.0” strategy, was credited for integrating content and technology to create new fan engagement experiences. CEO Lee was also acknowledged for playing a key role in the launch of Hybe China and Hybe India, accelerating the company’s multi-market strategy.

Billboard noted the strategic importance of establishing regional headquarters in the world’s two most populous countries, adding that Hybe artists Seventeen, Tomorrow X Together and Enhypen have all found success on the Billboard charts. According to Hybe, Seventeen topped the Top Album Sales chart with their fifth album, “Happy Burstday,” while Tomorrow X Together and Enhypen each reached No. 3 on the Billboard 200.

Hybe America CEO and chairman Isaac Lee was spotlighted for leading Hybe’s North American and Latin American expansion. Billboard noted that the company launched the boy band Santos Bravos through a Latin American reality series — a hybrid model combining K-pop production systems with Latin pop.

In addition to their appearance on the Leaderboard, the three Hybe executives also ranked fifth in the “Multisector” category, which evaluates influence across various parts of music-making and consumption — such as artist management and live entertainment.

Other executives named to this year’s Billboard Power 100 list include chairman and CEO Lucian Grainge of Universal Music Group, chairman Rob Stringer of Sony Music Group and president and CEO Michael Rapino of Live Nation.

# HYBE CEO: 2026 Marks HYBE 2.0 Realization Year

CEO outlines five tasks to transition HYBE 2.0 from investment to tangible results

By OSEN

Published 2026.01.02. 10:45

![](https://www.chosun.com/resizer/v2/GE3DAMRRHEZGENLFG4ZTOMBQGY.jpg?auth=2d1a6efc26e29ac89a4a89858f290d0202faaa27f85ab388070b36ce5f95bf31&width=530&height=742&smart=true)

[OSEN=Seon Mi-kyung Reporter] **“If 2025 was the year of ‘investment’ that laid the strategic foundation for ‘HYBE 2.0,’ then 2026 will mark the beginning of ‘realization’ where those results start to become visible.”**

Lee Jae-sang, CEO of HYBE, stated this in his New Year’s address on the 2nd, signaling that this year will be a turning point where the HYBE 2.0 strategy begins to yield tangible results. The CEO emphasized, “For us, 2025 was a period of strategic investment and strengthening our foundation for sustainable growth. This year will demonstrate to the market that our bold preemptive investments were the right direction and that the period of perseverance for improving our foundation was valuable.”

He then presented five key tasks for all members to achieve together this year: ▲ verifying the business viability of new businesses and IPs ▲ establishing the permanence of IPs and expanding the fan base through ‘unprecedented innovation’ ▲ designing new experience models based on scarcity ▲ securing leadership in the AI-based prosumer market ▲ and realizing true global governance.

First, the CEO highlighted the need to prove the validity of preemptive investments in new businesses and artist IPs. He explained, “We have already confirmed meaningful traffic growth, and now we must transition to a phase of generating valid revenue to ensure sustainable business viability. When investments yield results, new investments gain legitimacy, and a virtuous cycle of growth is established.”

He added, “The essence of HYBE’s business lies in achieving preemptive innovation through the fusion of content and technology, based on core IPs such as music and artists, and offering fans immersive experiences in new ways. This means laying the groundwork for HYBE artists to exist as unique IPs with the highest completeness and sustainability, even amid environmental changes.”

He also stressed the importance of designing new experience models based on scarcity. “Scarcity is a critical factor in enhancing the added value of fandom business and fan experiences,” he said. “2026 will be a pivotal year where HYBE, as it did in the past, designs and tests integrated online-offline experience models based on scarcity to innovate future music fandom business models.”

Securing leadership in the prosumer market, where fans and users directly produce and exchange content, was also deemed crucial. The CEO explained, “An integrated and concrete response model is needed from both creative and platform perspectives. By establishing a ‘Fan to Fan’ model where fans freely create and communicate while HYBE proactively protects IP value, we can set the market standard.”

Regarding global strategy, he stated, “We will build an integrated governance system that organically shares core assets and know-how—such as resources, personnel, and content distribution—among global subsidiaries to increase cross-border project cases and realize true global synergy.”

Finally, the CEO urged, “HYBE’s growth philosophy is clear: creating top-tier IPs, leading the fandom business, and expanding those achievements into new markets and genres. The protagonists who will lead this journey are all of us. I hope each HYBE member becomes a ‘driver and partner of growth.’” /seon@osen.co.kr
